violations.claim: VIOLATION: [DRY RUN] output is a false preview — dry-run path skips issueExists(),
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 149
; VIOLATION: [DRY RUN] output is a false preview — dry-run path skips issueExists(),
; so it prints and counts issues the non-dry-run path would silently skip as duplicates.
; This is an ordering constraint violation: the full skip logic is not applied uniformly.
(declare-const issue_already_on_github Int)
(declare-const is_dry_run Int)
(declare-const printed_as_dry_run_candidate Int)
(declare-const non_dry_run_would_file Int)
; Code transition: dry-run path unconditionally prints without calling issueExists()
(assert (= is_dry_run 1))
(assert (= printed_as_dry_run_candidate 1))
; Code transition: non-dry-run path calls issueExists() and skips on true return
; If the issue exists on GitHub, non-dry-run would enter the SKIP branch, not file
(assert (=> (= issue_already_on_github 1) (= non_dry_run_would_file 0)))
; Issue genuinely exists on GitHub
(assert (= issue_already_on_github 1))
; Violation: shown as [DRY RUN] candidate but real path would skip it
(assert (= printed_as_dry_run_candidate 1))
(assert (= non_dry_run_would_file 0))
(check-sat)

; Violation: shown as [DRY RUN] candidate but real path would skip it

(assert (= non_dry_run_would_file 0))
